title: Transferring App Ownership
excerpt: Hey there.  I’m working on switching my company over to a new monday account.  I have apps listed on the marketplace and a few in development in the account I’m currently using.  Is there a way to transfer the ownership …

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there. I’m working on switching my company over to a new monday account. I have apps listed on the marketplace and a few in development in the account I’m currently using. Is there a way to transfer the ownership of those live apps to another monday account.

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: @Matias.Monday I wanted to bump this. This is pretty important for me. Sorry to bother.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @liambailey,

As of today, it is not possible to transfer apps from one account to a different one.

Would you like for me to switch this to be a feature request?

Looking forward to hearing from you

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the reply. I would like it to be a feature request. Thanks.

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @liambailey - just converted it to one!

posts.author: PluginGenie (Oliver Albrecht)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that this feature is really important.

This is especially important if another company wanted to acquire just a single app from a developer. Currently, they would need to acquire all the apps, since there’s no way to separate them.

For developers creating new apps, this limitation makes it so that it’s best to create a separate developer account for every app to keep them separated.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @PluginGenie for the feedback!
